title: vauquelinia corymbosa subsp. angustifolia
intro: Vauquelinia corymbosa subsp. angustifolia, also known as the 'Vauquelinia' or 'Mormon Tea', is a plant species native to the southwestern United States and Mexico. It has been used for its medicinal properties, primarily due to its high content of flavonoids and phenolic acids, which exhibit antioxidant and anti-inflammatory activities. The plant is said to have various health benefits, including reducing fever, aiding in digestion, and acting as an expectorant to relieve respiratory issues. Vauquelinia corymbosa subsp. angustifolia can be prepared as a tea by infusing the leaves in hot water, and it has also been traditionally used in herbal remedies for its purported medicinal properties.
